behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at a module boundary (classifier -> API client payload). A pure unit test of a request-builder function can miss wiring bugs where the built taxonomy is not actually sent. No real OS/network/subprocess is required because an in-process fake transport can capture the outgoing payload. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: behavior text is about request construction content, not live network effects, model judgment, or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier + real request-construction code to an in-process fake API client that records requests. Call classify(document, taxonomy) and assert the sent payload contains the full taxonomy label list exactly as configured.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in response handoff (API response parsing + taxonomy validation + module return). An in-process stub API returning an out-of-taxonomy label is enough to catch real forwarding bugs. No real infra or model-quality dependence is required. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text fixes the returned label value for a deterministic invalid response, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process fake API that returns a raw label not present in taxonomy (e.g., "medical"). Invoke classify and assert module returns "unknown" to caller, not the raw label.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets deterministic normalization logic for identical inputs. This is a single-function/object boundary with no required I/O. Rejected higher levels: behavior text says "identical raw API responses" and "normalization step," which disqualifies needing live API, OS/network, or agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture(s) and taxonomy; assert output label is identical across runs and matches expected canonical mapping.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI calls and checks consistency tied to provider output. Mocking hides model/provider variability, so real model calls are needed. Rejected Workflow: although repeated calls occur, this is one agentic decision pattern repeated, not a multi-stage agent pipeline with distinct model-governed handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: Run N live classifications for the same document/taxonomy. Record raw provider response and final label each run. Assert: whenever raw provider response is unchanged, final label is unchanged; label changes are only allowed when provider response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration between token-budget policy and dispatch path (must enforce before send). In-process wiring with fake API client can catch real failures (sent when should reject, wrong truncation before send). Rejected System: no real OS/network behavior is required by the text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: this tests pre-dispatch policy enforcement, not model output quality or multi-step agent flow.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small token budget and known policy (truncate or reject). Use classifier wired to fake API transport. Submit over-budget document; assert policy is applied and dispatch behavior matches policy (truncated payload sent or request blocked).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real provider/model-side token accounting from live AI API responses; mocks can hide true provider usage behavior. Even with deterministic assertions, execution through real AI service is required to catch model/provider-accounting regressions. Rejected Workflow: behavior is a single-call budget check, not multiple agentic stages with handoff logic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification on representative document(s) using production model settings. Read provider-reported token usage metadata from response and assert usage <= configured budget. Repeat enough samples to detect drift/regression in provider accounting.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in retry/backoff orchestration across modules (error handling, wait policy, retry dispatch). An in-process fake API returning rate-limit then success can expose real logic bugs without real network. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network timing primitives; retry semantics can be validated with fake clock/stub. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on model output quality or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with fake API: first response is rate-limit signal, second is success. Use controllable clock/timer. Assert module waits per policy, retries, and returns successful classification instead of surfacing immediate error.
